title: salix × lavergnei
intro: Salix × lavergnei is a hybrid plant species within the willow family (Salicaceae) that is a cross between the white willow (Salix alba) and the Goosenose Willow (Salix × sepulcralis 'Lavergne'). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Salix × lavergnei include its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as arthritis, fever, and headaches. Herbal preparations of Salix × lavergnei can be made into teas, tinctures, or capsules, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
